name: Newar
notes: Newar (Nepal Bhasa, नेपाल भाषा) of the Kathmandu Valley, written today in Devanagari (historically in the Ranjana and other Nepalese scripts). The grapheme inventory is NOT yet specified: Devanagari is an abugida with an inherent vowel, dependent vowel signs, conjuncts and the virama, and the Newar sound system (including its tonal/phonation and vowel-length contrasts) does not map to the Devanagari letters the same way Nepali or Hindi does. Metadata only.
